title: Listening - Part 3: Part-time Jobs
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they chose a particular part-time job. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for choosing the job. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they chose a particular part-time job. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for choosing the job. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five young people talk about choosing a part-time job.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I’ve wanted to become a vet since I was little, although I realise it’s a difficult course to get into. When a weekend position appeared at a local animal centre, the pay wasn’t especially high and cleaning cages isn’t glamorous. [19]I applied because I thought experience of caring for animals would be valuable when I apply to study veterinary medicine later.[/19] I’ve learned a lot from the nurses there, and even the messy jobs seem worthwhile.||Speaker 2 Man: Most of my friends work during the holidays and spend the money on clothes or going out. I got a job stacking shelves in a supermarket, which isn’t particularly exciting, but the hours are reliable. [20]I’m putting nearly everything I earn towards a good camera, because photography has become my main hobby and my phone no longer gives me the quality I want.[/20] It may take all summer, but that makes me more determined not to waste my wages.||Speaker 3 Woman: I play basketball for my college team, and training is on three evenings a week, with matches most Saturdays. Several cafés offered me weekend shifts, but they would have meant missing games. [21]The cinema needed staff late on Friday and Sunday evenings, so it was the one job whose working hours didn’t interfere with my basketball commitments.[/21] I enjoy seeing new films too, but that was really an extra benefit rather than my main reason for accepting the job.||Speaker 4 Man: I work in a small gift shop near the harbour during the summer. I could probably earn more in my uncle’s restaurant, and the shop can be quiet when it rains. [22]What attracted me was that cruise ships stop nearby, so I spend much of the day speaking to customers from all over the world and hearing about where they come from.[/22] It’s made me much more confident about starting conversations with strangers.||Speaker 5 Woman: When my best friend started working at a busy bakery, she said they needed another person on Saturday mornings. I was nervous about taking orders because I’d never worked before, and getting up early is still difficult. [23]I agreed mainly because my friend would be there during every shift and could show me what to do until I got used to everything.[/23] Now I’m comfortable with customers, although we hardly have time to chat when the shop is full.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I’ve wanted to become a vet since I was little, although I realise it’s a difficult course to get into. When a weekend position appeared at a local animal centre, the pay wasn’t especially high and cleaning cages isn’t glamorous. I applied because I thought experience of caring for animals would be valuable when I apply to study veterinary medicine later. I’ve learned a lot from the nurses there, and even the messy jobs seem worthwhile.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: Most of my friends work during the holidays and spend the money on clothes or going out. I got a job stacking shelves in a supermarket, which isn’t particularly exciting, but the hours are reliable. I’m putting nearly everything I earn towards a good camera, because photography has become my main hobby and my phone no longer gives me the quality I want. It may take all summer, but that makes me more determined not to waste my wages.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: I play basketball for my college team, and training is on three evenings a week, with matches most Saturdays. Several cafés offered me weekend shifts, but they would have meant missing games. The cinema needed staff late on Friday and Sunday evenings, so it was the one job whose working hours didn’t interfere with my basketball commitments. I enjoy seeing new films too, but that was really an extra benefit rather than my main reason for accepting the job.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I work in a small gift shop near the harbour during the summer. I could probably earn more in my uncle’s restaurant, and the shop can be quiet when it rains. What attracted me was that cruise ships stop nearby, so I spend much of the day speaking to customers from all over the world and hearing about where they come from. It’s made me much more confident about starting conversations with strangers.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: When my best friend started working at a busy bakery, she said they needed another person on Saturday mornings. I was nervous about taking orders because I’d never worked before, and getting up early is still difficult. I agreed mainly because my friend would be there during every shift and could show me what to do until I got used to everything. Now I’m comfortable with customers, although we hardly have time to chat when the shop is full.

options.text: to practise a subject they study
to gain experience for a future career
to fit work around another commitment
to work with someone they know
to save for a particular purchase
to meet visitors from different countries
to provide a service for local people

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker chose the animal-centre job to gain relevant experience before applying to study veterinary medicine.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker is doing the supermarket job specifically to save for a camera.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The cinema shifts allow the speaker to continue attending basketball training and matches.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker chose a harbour gift shop in order to meet international visitors.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker accepted the bakery job because her friend would be working alongside her.
